title: Yom Argo
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Argo.jpg]] |name=Yom "Dart" Argo |homeworld=[[Telfrey]]{{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}} |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], [[Lahsbane/Legends|Lahsbane]][[Star Wars (1977) 73|''Star Wars'' (1977) 73]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|We don't seem to be getting through to these ''Lahsbees'' at all, ''See-Threepio.'' Are you sure you're translating everything I say correctly?"
"Absolutely, ''Master Luke!'' They quite readily admit that one of the missing Rebel pilots we're searching for—''Yom Argo''—crashlanded and died here on Lahsbane.|Luke Skywalker and C-3PO|}} '''Yom Argo''', also known as '''Dart''', was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] who served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A native of [[Telfrey]], Argo and his partner [[Tay Vanis]] drew [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] ire for fomenting rebellious sentiment in the [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]], leading to Telfrey's [[Decimation of Telfrey|destruction]]. Argo and Vanis later received [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]]-sourced intelligence regarding a new Imperial [[Death Star II/Legends|superweapon]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]—however, Argo [[Death/Legends|died]] in a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] crash on [[Lahsbane/Legends|Lahsbane]] before making it back to the Rebel [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|The Imperials probably already know about Argo's crash, and if they suspect that records of his mission might exist—like ''our'' leaders in the ''Rebel Alliance'' do—they may come looking for them. And they won't ask nicely.|Luke Skywalker|}} A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Telfrey]], Yom Argo—nicknamed "Dart"—{{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}} joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] as a [[Spy/Legends|spy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],[[Star Wars (1977) 77|''Star Wars'' (1977) 77]] working closely with his friend and countryman [[Tay Vanis]]. Argo and Vanis earned [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] notice for fomenting Rebel sentiment throughout their native [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]], including among the aquatic [[Iskalonian]] [[The School|School]] on [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]]—the rising unrest in the system caused a brutal Imperial crackdown. By the end, Argo and Vanis' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Telfrey had been rendered completely uninhabitable. Around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Argo and Vanis received [[Death Star II plans|information]] from [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] spies[[Star Wars (1977) 80|''Star Wars'' (1977) 80]] about a new [[Death Star II/Legends|superweapon]] under development by the Empire, and downloaded the intelligence onto tapes they intended to deliver to Rebel leadership. Argo and Vanis quickly realized that the Empire was in hot pursuit, and the old friends decided their best course of action was to split up: Vanis would take the tapes and flee, while Argo returned to request aid from the Rebel [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. Aided by a member of the diminutive [[Lahsbee/Huhk|Lahsbee]] [[Species/Legends|species]], Argo traveled to the Lahsbee homeworld of [[Lahsbane/Legends|Lahsbane]], but his [[Starship/Legends|starship]] crashed and the Rebel was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the impact. To honor his Lahsbee companion, a set of tapes that contained a record of their mission was taken to the Lahsbee [[The Forbidden City|Forbidden City]] and placed in a memorial. His friend Vanis also disappeared before making it back to a Rebel base—he was later found to have been captured by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] into a vegetative state. The circumstances of their disappearance, and the whereabouts of the Bothan tapes, immediately became one of the Alliance's utmost priorities, prompting the Rebels to dispatch a group including top leaders [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] to discover their fate and retrieve the intelligence. Their search first led them to Lahsbane, where they learned from the native Lahsbees that Argo had died, and proceeded to retrieve his tapes from the Forbidden City. ==Personality and traits== Yom Argo was a Human male with [[Color/Legends|black]] hair. ==Behind the scenes== Yom Argo was first mentioned in [[Star Wars (1977) 73|''Star Wars'' (1977) 73]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] and released in [[1983]], and was mentioned several times in subsequent issues of the original [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'' comic]]. Argo was briefly pictured alongside companion Tay Vanis in [[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]], where he was illustrated by [[Ron Frenz]], although the comic does not identify which one is which. That question was later cleared up by the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] over two decades later.{{DB|character|yomargo|Argo, Yom "Dart"}} ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 73|''Star Wars'' (1977) 73]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 73}} {{1stm}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 74}} {{1stp}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 77|''Star Wars'' (1977) 77]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 77}} {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 80|''Star Wars'' (1977) 80]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 80}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{CollectorCite|5|ComicScan|ComicScan: 20 Tales to Tantalize}} *{{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|yomargo|Argo, Yom "Dart"}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Argo, Yom}} [[Category:Alliance Starfighter Corps personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]]
